Best Laptops of 2026: Top Picks Tested by CNET
These are the best laptops my colleagues and I have reviewed, from basic models to high-powered gaming systems and everything in between.
Our Experts
Written by
Matt Elliott
Article updated on
April 3, 2026 at 10:57 AM PDT
Matt Elliott
Senior Editor
Matt Elliott is a senior editor at CNET with a focus on laptops and streaming services. Matt has more than 20 years of experience testing and reviewing laptops. He has worked for CNET in New York and San Francisco and now lives in New Hampshire. When he's not writing about laptops, Matt likes to play and watch sports. He loves to play tennis and hates the number of streaming services he has to subscribe to in order to watch the various sports he wants to watch.
Expertise
Laptops | Desktops | All-in-one PCs | Streaming devices | Streaming platforms

vs
Compare
Back to selection
I’ve tested a wide range of laptops in the past few months and have favorites across a broad range of categories, from sleek, long-lasting ultraportables and MacBooks to high-powered laptops for gamers and creators. The two newest additions are a study in contrast, with the budget
MacBook Neo
and the over-the-top
Alienware 18 Area-51
gaming laptop. The Neo is the best $600 laptop you can buy, and the 18-inch Area-51 I tested costs more than $5,000 but is a wonderland of RGB lights and laptop tech.
Our Picks
Best laptop overall
M5 MacBook Air (15-inch, 2026)
$1,249 at Amazon
9.0/10
CNET Score
CNET Score
CNET provides expert, unbiased reviews of products and services. When we assign a score, we use a scale of 1-10. Each product we score is evaluated by criteria specific to its category with most assessing pricing, quality, features and performance.
Read more on: How we test
The 15-inch MacBook Air is proof that you don't need a Pro to get a larger display. It supplies a larger screen that you once found only on the pricier Pro models. If you're eyeing the 14- or 16-inch Pro models primarily for the added screen size, the MacBook Air 15 is the more affordable option you should go for.
Read full review
Jump to details

Optimal balance of screen size and system weight

Smooth ProMotion display still exclusive to MacBook Pro

Matt Elliott is a senior editor at CNET with a focus on laptops and streaming services. Matt has more than 20 years of experience testing and reviewing laptops. He has worked for CNET in New York and San Francisco and now lives in New Hampshire. When he's not writing about laptops, Matt likes to play and watch sports. He loves to play tennis and hates the number of streaming services he has to subscribe to in order to watch the various sports he wants to watch.
Expertise
Laptops | Desktops | All-in-one PCs | Streaming devices | Streaming platforms
See full bio
Why You Can Trust CNET
30+

35
Product Reviewers

CNET staff -- not advertisers, partners or business interests -- determine how we review products and services. If you buy through our links, we may earn a commission.
Reviews ethics statement
How we test
vs

Back to selection
I’ve tested a wide range of laptops in the past few months and have favorites across a broad range of categories, from sleek, long-lasting ultraportables and MacBooks to high-powered laptops for gamers and creators. The two newest additions are a study in contrast, with the budget
MacBook Neo
and the over-the-top
Alienware 18 Area-51
gaming laptop. The Neo is the best $600 laptop you can buy, and the 18-inch Area-51 I tested costs more than $5,000 but is a wonderland of RGB lights and laptop tech.
Our Picks
Best laptop overall
M5 MacBook Air (15-inch, 2026)
$1,249 at Amazon
9.0/10
CNET Score
CNET Score
CNET provides expert, unbiased reviews of products and services. When we assign a score, we use a scale of 1-10. Each product we score is evaluated by criteria specific to its category with most assessing pricing, quality, features and performance.
Read more on: How we test
The 15-inch MacBook Air is proof that you don't need a Pro to get a larger display. It supplies a larger screen that you once found only on the pricier Pro models. If you're eyeing the 14- or 16-inch Pro models primarily for the added screen size, the MacBook Air 15 is the more affordable option you should go for.

5 Best laptops for every budget this 2026 - TechEngage

Laptops
Photo by Ash Edmonds on Unsplash
Facebook
Tweet

Selecting a laptop can be daunting because of the numerous models and versions available in the market. Every year, there are new innovations like super retina displays and edge-to-edge keyboards. Although it may be tempting to purchase the latest models, the truth is that you may not require all the fancy features.
So whether you’re a gamer, marketing professional, or like to browse for leisure, here are the best laptops for every budget coming out this 2026.
Table of Contents
Laptop
Pros
Cons
HP Chromebook 14
Affordable, reliable performance for everyday tasks
Limited storage space, may not be suitable for demanding tasks
Google Pixelbook Go
Stylish design, powerful processor, long battery life
Relatively higher price compared to other Chromebooks
Dell XPS 13
Powerful performance, stunning display
Higher price range compared to budget laptops
Apple MacBook Pro
Retina display, faster performance, improved keyboard
Higher price range, limited customization options
Alienware Area-51m
Most powerful gaming laptop, customizable hardware
Expensive, may be overkill for casual users or non-gamers
HP Chromebook 14
HP Chromebook 14 / via HP

If you’re looking for a laptop that can browse, run Microsoft Office, and perform other basic activities, you can buy a Chromebook. At a very affordable price, the HP Chromebook 14 already features an Intel Celeron processor and HD Graphics 500 card — the standard for any decent laptop. When you put it side-by-side with HP’s other Windows 10-powered laptops, you’ll find that the HP Chromebook 14 offers similar specs besides graphics and memory.
The HP Chromebook 14 is a budget-friendly laptop that offers impressive performance for everyday tasks and basic activities. This Chromebook stands out for its affordability and reliable performance and is designed for users who need a portable device for browsing, running Microsoft Office, and other essential functions.
Key Features
Processor and Graphics:
The HP Chromebook 14 is equipped with an Intel Celeron processor and HD Graphics 500 card, providing a seamless user experience and smooth multitasking capabilities. It delivers efficient performance for everyday computing needs.
Display:
The Chromebook features a 14-inch display with a resolution that offers clear visuals for web browsing, media consumption, and document editing. While it may not have the highest resolution on the market, it is certainly more than sufficient for everyday use.
Operating System:
As a Chromebook, it runs on Google’s Chrome OS, which is known for its simplicity and seamless integration with Google s
